title: Today aggregator GH Action
description:
GH Actions workflow that runs every minute and produces the ranked Today feed.

Steps:
1. Fetch all channel posts for today from export branch (raw.githubusercontent.com)
2. Fetch all vote comments from khorshid-social Issues for today's Issues (plaintext, no decryption needed)
3. Fetch all flag comments similarly
4. LLM deduplication: POST today's channel post headlines + timestamps to Claude Haiku API. Ask it to return cluster_id per post. Posts in the same cluster are the same story.
5. Tally importance votes per post (deduplicated by vote_id)
6. Hot score each post: wilson_lower_bound(votes, total_seen) * exp(-0.3 * age_hours)
7. Hot score room posts: same formula
8. Detect elevated comments: comments with hot_score exceeding threshold → tagged as Community Report
9. Apply flags: content with ≥ N unique flag vote_ids → excluded
10. Write feed/{day}.json to export branch:
    { posts: [...sorted by hot_score], community_reports: [...], generated_at: ... }

Needs: CLAUDE_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN (auto-provisioned) repo secrets.
Claude model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (cheapest, fast enough for headline clustering)
close_reason: Implemented: mirror/aggregate.ts + mirror/feed-schema.ts + .github/workflows/aggregator.yml. Runs every 5 min, clusters via GPT-4o mini on GitHub Models (no extra secret), simple decay scoring, flag hiding at ≥5, top-3 community reports.

title: GitHub Issues social schema — comments, votes, flags
close_reason: social/schema.ts defines all wire format types; just social-check passes tsc strict+noUncheckedIndexedAccess with 0 errors

title: Create khorshid-social repo and daily Issue provisioning
description:
Set up the social layer infrastructure.

What needs to be created:
- Public GitHub repo: MaroMushii/khorshid-social
- GH Actions workflow in khorshid-social that runs every minute and ensures today's Issues exist for all active channels and rooms

Issue naming convention:
  channel-<slug>-<YYYY-MM-DD>   e.g. channel-bbcpersian-2026-05-11
  room-<slug>-<YYYY-MM-DD>      e.g. room-street-reports-2026-05-11

Issue creation logic:
  for each active channel/room:
    title = '{type}-{slug}-{today}'
    if no Issue with that title exists: create it with appropriate label
    cache issue number in a manifest file for fast client lookup

Labels: one per channel/room slug + 'channel' or 'room' type label

PAT needed: KHORSHID_SOCIAL_PAT repo secret with issues:write on khorshid-social.
close_reason: MaroMushii/khorshid-social created and live. provision.yml running every minute — 9 channel Issues + manifest.json verified. Channels hardcoded (Khorshid-849 will update). justfile provision-social recipe added.

title: Sign writes + verify reads — Ed25519 content authentication
description:
Every write to Khorshid-Social must be signed with the author's Ed25519 private key. Every read must verify the signature before decrypting. Unsigned or invalid content is silently dropped.

Wire format change (v:2):
  { v: 2, n: '<nonce b64>', c: '<ciphertext b64>', pub: '<pubkey hex>', sig: '<Ed25519 sig over nonce||ciphertext, b64>' }

Write path (mac):
  After encrypting, sign (nonce_bytes || ciphertext_bytes) with IdentityStore.sign()
  Include pub as identity.publicKeyHex
  Include sig as base64(signature)

Read path (mac):
  For each comment: parse JSON, verify Ed25519(pub, nonce||ciphertext, sig) before AES-GCM decrypt
  Drop anything missing pub/sig or with invalid signature — no error shown to user

Why this matters:
  Khorshid-Social is a public repo — any GitHub account can post to its Issues without using the app PATs at all. Structural validation and AES-GCM auth tags already drop random garbage, but they do not prevent a saboteur who reverse-engineers the wire format from posting plausible-looking content. Signature verification closes this gap: only content from a valid app identity (a real keypair generated on-device) will be rendered.

Depends on: Ed25519 identity (Khorshid-aqh), comment system (Khorshid-pye)
close_reason: SocialPayloadWrapper v:2 with pub+sig fields. SocialCrypto: signatureMessage/applySignature helpers, Ed25519 verify in decrypt() for v:2, hex decode helper. SocialStore.send() signs every write with IdentityStore. Build passes Swift 6. Khorshid-aqh macOS slice already shipped.

title: Voting system via GitHub Issues API
description:
Upvote and importance signal for channel posts and room posts/comments.

Write path:
1. User taps vote
2. Compute vote_id = sha256(local_private_key || target_id) — commitment hash, no pubkey exposed
3. Construct plaintext: { type: 'vote', target_id, signal: 'up'|'important', vote_id, sent_at }
4. POST as Issue comment (plaintext JSON, NOT encrypted)
5. Optimistic UI: vote count increments immediately

Read path:
- Votes arrive via same polling loop as comments
- Identified by type='vote' field (no decrypt needed)
- Tally per target_id, deduplicate by vote_id
- 'important' votes feed into Today aggregator hot scoring

vote_id privacy model:
- Same user + same target → same hash (deduplication)
- Same user + different targets → different hashes (no cross-post correlation)
- Cannot be reversed to reveal voter identity

The Today aggregator reads these same plaintext vote comments to rank content in feed/{day}.json.
close_reason: Voting system shipped — VoteTally model, vote()/hasVoted() on SocialStore, poll tally with vote_id dedup, optimistic UI with undo on failure

title: Comment system via GitHub Issues API
description:
Read and write comments on room posts via GitHub Issues API. Channel posts (Telegram news) are vote-only — no comment threads on channels.

Write path:
1. User writes comment in a room
2. Construct plaintext: { type: 'comment', post_id, reply_to, body, sent_at }
3. AES-256-GCM encrypt with room key → { v, n, c }
4. Pick random PAT from pool
5. POST /repos/MaroMushii/khorshid-social/issues/:id/comments
6. Optimistic UI: comment appears locally as pending
7. Confirmed on next poll when it appears in API response

Read path:
1. On room open: GET /repos/.../issues?labels=<slug>&state=open, find today's issue by title, cache issue number
2. GET /repos/.../issues/:id/comments (initial load)
3. Poll: GET /repos/.../issues/:id/comments?since=<last_seen>
4. For each new comment: try AES-GCM decrypt; if type='comment': render
5. Adaptive interval: 10s when user is active, 60s backgrounded

reply_to field is sha256 of parent comment's ciphertext — used for threaded display.

Depends on: PAT pool (Khorshid-il6), khorshid-social setup (Khorshid-x2x)
notes: Started. Branch: feat/comment-system. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-pye.md — rooms not yet in manifest; issueNotFound handled silently
close_reason: SocialClient actor, SocialCrypto AES-GCM helpers, SocialStore polling + optimistic UI, SocialPayload/Comment models all live. Build passes under Swift 6. Room issues not yet in manifest — handled gracefully.

title: Deploy Cloudflare dispatcher for reliable cron
description:
The GH Actions free-tier scheduled workflows fire at ~6% of the configured rate. The cf-dispatcher Worker fixes this.

Steps:
1. cd cf-dispatcher && pnpm install
2. pnpm exec wrangler login (as MaroMushii CF account)
3. just deploy-dispatcher
4. just set-dispatcher-token  — paste a fine-grained PAT scoped to MaroMushii/Khorshid (Actions: read+write)
5. Verify in CF dashboard: Worker > Triggers > Cron Triggers shows */5

Monitor: wrangler tail khorshid-mirror-dispatcher to confirm dispatches are landing.
notes: Started. Ops-only — no branch. Wrangler auth expired; re-login required before deploy. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-6p0.md
close_reason: Cloudflare is blocked in Iran client-side. Pivoting to GitHub-only. CF dispatcher no longer needed — GH Actions built-in cron is sufficient.

Expand channels.json beyond the initial 9.

Current list: bbcpersian, dwpersian, farsi_iranwire, farsivoa, hranews, iranintltv, mamlekate, radiofarda, sahamnewsorg

Candidates to evaluate and add:

Process: verify each channel is active, has consistent posts, not a spam/disinformation vector. Add to channels.json via PR.

title: Private rooms — invite-only encrypted group discussion
description:
Invite-only encrypted group discussion. Each private room is a separate private GitHub repo.

Design (mirrors Curvy sister project):
- Room creator needs a GitHub account
- Creator creates a private repo (khorshid-room-<random-id>) and mints a fine-grained PAT scoped to that repo (issues:write)
- Invite bundle: { owner, repo, pat, key, name } — base64 encoded, shared as QR code or paste
- Room members don't need GitHub accounts — they write as an anonymous bot via the shared PAT
- Same GitHub Issues API pattern as public rooms: one Issue per day, Issue comments are encrypted AES-GCM blobs
- Room key never leaves Keychain after paste

Invite bundle flow:
  Creator: generate room key, create private repo, mint PAT, encode bundle, share via QR
  Joiner: paste bundle into app, app verifies repo access via GitHub API, stores in Keychain

The room key is the privacy guarantee, not repo ACL (though private repos add metadata protection).

title: PAT pool — anonymous write tokens for public users
description:
Users don't need GitHub accounts to write to public channels or rooms.

The app ships a set of fine-grained PATs created by the project owner (MaroMushii), scoped to issues:write on MaroMushii/khorshid-social. GitHub allows many PATs per account — each gets its own 5,000 req/hour rate limit bucket. Pool 5-10 PATs for ample capacity.

Storage:
- Set hardcoded in app binary (fallback if fetch fails)
- Self-updating credential file at raw.githubusercontent.com/MaroMushii/Khorshid/refs/heads/main/pats.json
- App fetches on launch, caches locally, falls back to hardcoded set

Usage:
- App picks a random PAT per write operation
- On HTTP 429 (rate limit) or 401/403 (revoked): try next PAT in pool

Rotation: update pats.json in main branch. Apps pick up on next launch. No app update required.

Risk model: extracted PATs can write garbage (filtered by clients checking payload structure) but cannot impersonate anyone (no private key) and cannot read private rooms (no AES key).
close_reason: PATPool service implemented: remote fetch → UserDefaults cache → hardcoded fallback. Wired into KhorshidApp environment. pats.json schema at repo root.

title: Today's Highlights — daily curated feed sorted by importance votes
description:
The main editorial surface of Khorshid. Aggregates content from two sources:

1. Channel posts — top news from mirrored Telegram channels, ranked by importance votes
2. Room posts — hot community posts from curated rooms, ranked by same hot formula

LLM deduplication (Claude Haiku in GH Actions) clusters same stories across channels so the same event doesn't appear 5 times.

Hot scoring formula:
  hot_score = wilson_lower_bound(upvotes, total_seen) * exp(-λ * age_hours)
  λ = 0.3 for posts, λ = 0.5 for comments (comments go stale faster)

Community Report: comments that exceed the hot threshold are elevated to Today alongside news posts, with a 'Community Report' badge. This is the bottom-up path — eyewitness accounts can reach the same prominence as official news.

Community flagging: content flagged by ≥ N unique users (vote_id-based dedup) is hidden from Today.

GH Actions aggregator runs every minute, writes feed/{day}.json to the export branch. Client reads this as the primary Today source.
notes: Started. Branch: feat/today-highlights. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-0h1.md — aggregator (Khorshid-pen) already ships feed/<date>.json; this bead is mac client surface only
close_reason: All client surface shipped: FeedPost/Confirmation models, FeedClient, FeedStore with 60s polling + yesterday fallback, TodayFeedView/FeedPostRow/ConfirmationsBadge views, SidebarSelection refactor, FeedStore wired into KhorshidApp env. Merged feat/today-highlights.

title: Comment system — write and read comments via Firestore
description:
Implement commenting under news posts.

Write path:
- User writes comment → sign with Ed25519 → AES-GCM encrypt with room key → pick PAT from pool → write to Firestore + GitHub
- Comment document: { id (sha256 of payload), sender_pubkey, payload (ciphertext), nonce, sig, sent_at, reply_to? }

Read path:
- Firestore real-time listener on posts/{postId}/comments
- Decrypt with room key, verify signature
- Display as threaded list under each post (reply_to for nesting)

PAT pool needed for write path — block on that bead or implement with single hardcoded PAT for dev.
close_reason: Firestore dropped. Comment system redesigned around GitHub Issues API encrypted blobs.

title: Voting system — upvote posts and comments via Firestore
description:
Implement post and comment upvoting.

Write path:
- User taps upvote → sign vote payload with Ed25519 key → AES-GCM encrypt with room key → write to Firestore
- Vote document: { sender_pubkey, target_id, type: 'up'|'important', sig, sent_at }

Read path:
- Subscribe to Firestore votes collection for visible posts
- Aggregate client-side: count unique sender_pubkeys per post
- Surface 'importance' votes separately — these float posts to Today's Highlights

'Important' is a distinct signal from 'upvote': marks something as critical news people need to see, not just popular.
close_reason: Firestore dropped. Voting system redesigned around GitHub Issues API plaintext votes with commitment hash (vote_id = sha256(privkey||target_id)).

title: Ed25519 identity — keypair generation and local storage
description:
Implement the zero-signup identity layer, shared concept across both apps.

macOS (Swift):
- Generate Ed25519 keypair on first launch via CryptoKit
- Store private key in Keychain (service: dev.kumamaki.Khorshid)
- Public key is the user's identity — display name stored alongside it locally
- Export keypair as QR code for device migration

Android (Dart):
- Generate Ed25519 keypair using pointycastle or cryptography package
- Store in Android Keystore
- Same display name + public key model

No server-side registration. No GitHub account. No email.
notes: macOS slice done (IdentityStore, Keychain, sign(), voteId() — all in use by ro9/s7m/pye). Android half blocked on Khorshid-o5y (Flutter skeleton not yet built). Re-enter this bead once o5y ships.

title: Android app skeleton — Flutter + news feed from export branch
description:
Bootstrap the Flutter Android app under android/.

- flutter create with package name dev.kumamaki.khorshid
- MirrorRepository — fetches index.json + snapshots from raw.githubusercontent.com
- NewsProvider (Riverpod) — channels + posts state
- Basic scaffold: channel list drawer + post feed
- Post card: channel name, headline, timestamp, media thumbnail
- Polling every 5 min
- No GT proxy needed on Android (direct raw.githubusercontent.com fetch is sufficient for MVP)

No social layer yet — read-only news feed.

title: macOS app skeleton — xcodegen + news feed from export branch
description:
Bootstrap the SwiftUI macOS 26 app under mac/.

- project.yml with xcodegen config (bundle ID: dev.kumamaki.Khorshid)
- KhorshidApp.swift entry point
- MirrorClient — fetches index.json + channel snapshots from raw.githubusercontent.com/MaroMushii/Khorshid/refs/heads/export/
- ChannelStore (@Observable, @MainActor) — holds channels + posts
- Basic two-column NavigationSplitView: channel list left, post feed right
- Post row: channel name, headline, timestamp, view count, reactions
- Polling every 5 min (same cadence as mirror)
- GT proxy + pinned IPs for bypass (port from Pigeon's PinnedHTTPSClient + PinnedURLProtocol)

No social layer yet — read-only news feed.
notes: Started. Branch: feat/mac-app-skeleton. Plan: .beads/scratch/Khorshid-50o.md. GT proxy excluded (raw.githubusercontent.com whitelisted in Iran).

title: Design Firestore schema for comments and votes
description:
Define the Firestore collection structure for the social layer.

Collections needed:
- posts/{postId}/votes — per-post vote counts and voter pubkeys
- posts/{postId}/comments — encrypted comment blobs
- posts/{postId}/comments/{commentId}/votes — comment upvotes

Each document:
- signed with sender Ed25519 keypair
- AES-GCM encrypted with room key
- post_id references the Telegram post ID from the mirror snapshot (e.g. 'bbcpersian/1234')

Also spec out the daily aggregation format:
feed/{day}.json — posts sorted by vote count, written by GH Action
close_reason: Firestore dropped from design. Replaced by GitHub Issues schema bead.

title: Community Report — elevated comments in Today feed
description:
Hot comments surface in Today alongside official news posts.

Detection runs in the aggregator GH Action:
  for each comment in today's issues:
    hot_score = wilson_lower_bound(importance_votes, total_votes) * exp(-0.5 * age_hours)
    if hot_score > COMMUNITY_REPORT_THRESHOLD: mark as community_report

feed/{day}.json includes community_report entries verbatim (encrypted blob included)
so clients can decrypt and display without a separate API call. The aggregator never
reads comment content — only tallies plaintext vote counts.

UI in Today feed:
- Community Report cards appear in the ranked list alongside channel post cards
- Badge: 'Community Report' label with a people icon
- Same card shape as news posts — user content is first-class, not subordinate
- Tapping expands the comment inline (no separate navigation to comment thread)

This is the bottom-up path: a ground-level eyewitness account can reach the same
prominence in Today as an official BBC Persian article, if enough people mark it important.

title: Community flagging — threshold-based local content hiding
description:
Decentralized moderation: users flag room comments, clients hide them at threshold. Flags apply to user-generated room comments only — not to Telegram news posts.

Write path:
1. User flags a room comment
2. Compute vote_id = sha256(local_private_key || target_id)
3. POST plaintext: { type: 'flag', target_id, vote_id, sent_at } as Issue comment in the room's Issue
4. Optimistic: flag recorded locally

Read path:
- Flag comments arrive via polling loop (plaintext, type='flag')
- Tally unique vote_ids per target_id
- At threshold N (start with 5, tune later): hide comment from UI
- Hiding is client-local — content still exists on GitHub

Core team permanent removal: delete Issue comment directly via GitHub web UI or API.
Deleted comments stop appearing in subsequent GET /comments responses — all clients drop them on next poll.
